key_insight: Geoalign improves online LLM reinforcement learning by detecting and correcting batch-outlier rollouts whose reward-induced directions conflict with the majority consensus.
engineering_relevance: It is a lightweight, forward-pass-only curation plug-in that can stabilize policy optimization under noisy or misspecified rewards with negligible overhead.
method: Forms within-prompt preference pairs from rollouts.
Learns an online projector over per-rollout hidden states to align reward-ordered displacement directions.
Flags directionally inconsistent rollouts by angular deviation from a batch prototype and swaps in stable alternatives.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports gains on dialogue alignment with a learned reward model and mathematical reasoning with binary verified rewards, with reduced training oscillation and better final performance versus PF-PPO, PAR, PODS, and Seed-GRPO.
watch_item: The method depends on reliable within-prompt alternatives and a meaningful consensus direction, which may be harder to estimate in highly sparse, biased, or very small batches.
paper_title: GEOALIGN: Geometric Rollout Curation for Robust LLM Reinforcement Learning
